title: PCR-Based Early Warning Systems for Foodborne Outbreak Prevention
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Foodborne     outbreaks     remain     a     major     public     health     concern  ,   causing     significant     morbidity  ,   mortality  ,   and     economic     losses     worldwide  . Early   detection     of     pathogens     is     essential     to     prevent     large-scale     outbreaks     and     protect     food     security  . PCR-  based     technologies  ,   including   real-  time     and   digital PCR,   offer     sensitive  ,   specific  ,   and     rapid     identification     of     microbial     contaminants   in   food  .   Their     integration     into     national     surveillance     systems     can     dramatically     reduce     response     time     and     enable     timely     intervention  .   This     policy     brief     highlights     the     importance     of   PCR-  based     early     warning     systems     for     foodborne     pathogen     detection  .   It     provides     an     overview     of     current     challenges  ,   key     messages  ,   and     recommendations     to     strengthen     laboratory     capacity  ,   harmonize   testing   standards  ,   and     improve     outbreak     preparedness  ,   ultimately     safeguarding     public     health     and     consumer     confidence   in   the     food     supply     chain  .
